faq.question: How much does it cost to replace a window in San Francisco?
faq.answer: In San Francisco, with the current promotion quality vinyl replacement starts at $549 per window installed (regular $915), while fiberglass, clad-wood and architectural products price higher. The final number depends on window count, sizes, frame material and access, so Deluxe Windows prices each San Francisco project after on-site measurements.

faq.question: What is the average cost of a standard replacement window?
faq.answer: In San Francisco, with the current promotion quality vinyl replacement starts at $549 per window installed (regular $915), while fiberglass, clad-wood and architectural products price higher. The final number depends on window count, sizes, frame material and access, so Deluxe Windows prices each San Francisco project after on-site measurements. Ask about San Francisco specifics when booking the free estimate.

faq.question: Do you need a permit to replace windows in San Francisco?
faq.answer: Yes — San Francisco requires a building permit for every window replacement; in-kind swaps can now be permitted online. Deluxe Windows confirms the exact requirement with the local building department and handles the paperwork as part of the installation contract.

faq.question: Which window brands work best for San Francisco homes?
faq.answer: Popular choices in San Francisco include Milgard, Anlin and Simonton vinyl for value, and Marvin or Andersen for premium projects. The right pick depends on the home's age, style and exposure in San Francisco.

faq.question: How long does window replacement take in San Francisco?
faq.answer: Most San Francisco projects are measured in one visit and installed in one to three working days once windows arrive, typically four to eight weeks after ordering.
